channel.title: Data Breach & Ransomware Aggregator
channel.description: Real-time data breach notifications from state registries, federal sources, and ransomware trackers

item.title: FortiBleed Actors Collaborating With Inc, Lynx Ransomware Gangs
item.description:
After gaining a foothold in thousands of Fortinet firewalls, the attackers are starting to monetize that access, and are also piling on a Nextcloud zero-day bug.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Aussies Face Reduced Cybercrime Risk, as Pressure Shifts to SMBs
item.description:
Improved institutional safeguards and stricter regulations have pushed the burdens of protection and risk reduction on to Australian businesses.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Chinese LLMs Broaden the Gap Between Attackers & Defenders
item.description:
Two new models from Chinese firms compete with top US mainstream and frontier models. Should cyber-defenders be worried?
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: JadePuffer: The First Complete LLM-Driven Ransomware Attack
item.description:
An "agentic threat actor" successfully exploited a Langflow flaw to steal data from a production database server and encrypt other systems.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: CitrixBleed-ing Again? NetScaler Vulnerability Under Attack
item.description:
Attackers wasted little time targeting the latest memory disclosure flaw in Citrix's NetScaler products, after researchers published a proof-of-concept exploit (PoC).
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: 'GitLost' Flaw Leaks Private Data From GitHub's Agentic Workflows
item.description:
The flaw allows an unauthenticated attacker to craft a GitHub Issue in an org's public repository and then silently pull data from its private repos, too.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Dialogflow CX 'Rogue Agent' Flaw Enabled AI Chatbot Data Theft
item.description:
Varonis reported the flaw to Google in late 2025 and it has been addressed, but it reminds defenders to take a fresh look at their AI Infrastructure security.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Blogspot-Hosted Payloads Delivered in ‘Veil#Drop’ Attacks
item.description:
Securonix says the sophisticated framework abuses compromised websites, Blogspot, PowerShell, and fileless techniques to evade detection and deploy the PureLog information stealer. The post Blogspot-Hosted Payloads Delivered in ‘Veil#Drop’ Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Keyfactor Scores $1 Billion+ Investment for AI, Post-Quantum Security
item.description:
The investment will accelerate Keyfactor's machine identity, PKI, and cryptographic security platform as enterprises prepare for AI-driven and post-quantum threats. The post Keyfactor Scores $1 Billion+ Investment for AI, Post-Quantum Security appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Linux Kernel Vulnerability Allows VM Escape on Intel and AMD Systems
item.description:
The 16-year-old Januscape flaw affects Linux's KVM hypervisor, allowing attackers to escape virtual machines and potentially execute code on the underlying host. The post Linux Kernel Vulnerability Allows VM Escape on Intel and AMD Systems appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: CISO Conversations: Tarah Wheeler, Cybersecurity Leader, Thought Leader and Original Thinker
item.description:
Tarah Wheeler is CISO at TPO Group, a firm that provides cybersecurity consultancy for high-stakes organizations. But despite this elevated position, her journey was far from typical. The post CISO Conversations: Tarah Wheeler, Cybersecurity Leader, Thought Leader and Original Thinker appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Iran-Linked Hackers Using Modular C&C Framework in Cyberattacks
item.description:
Researchers say the Iran-linked threat actor used an adaptable modular malware framework and compromised IT service providers to reach high-value targets in Israel. The post Iran-Linked Hackers Using Modular C&C Framework in Cyberattacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Critical Adobe ColdFusion Vulnerability Exploited in Attacks
item.description:
Hackers are exploiting a recently patched critical vulnerability (CVE-2026-48282) in Adobe ColdFusion that carries a CVSS score of 10/10. The post Critical Adobe ColdFusion Vulnerability Exploited in Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Critical Gitea Flaw Under Active Exploitation, Researchers Warn
item.description:
Attackers are exploiting the critical Gitea vulnerability CVE-2026-20896 to bypass authentication with a single HTTP header and access vulnerable repositories and secrets. The post Critical Gitea Flaw Under Active Exploitation, Researchers Warn appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: County Government Reportedly Paid $1 Million to Cyber Extortion Group
item.description:
The alleged victim, believed to be a small Ohio county, reportedly paid the extortion group to prevent the public release of sensitive stolen data. The post County Government Reportedly Paid $1 Million to Cyber Extortion Group appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Canadian spy agency reports hacking three criminal groups in 2025
item.description:
A ransomware-as-a-service gang, an online foreign extremist group and drug traffickers were separately the targets of offensive operations in 2025, according to Canada's Communications Security Establishment.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: UK cyber pledge draws only a handful of top firms despite ministerial appeal
item.description:
Those that did sign include large firms such as Aviva, the London Stock Exchange Group and Marks & Spencer, which lost hundreds of millions of pounds in a cyberattack last year, as well as small cybersecurity consultancies.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Major Japanese telco says cyberattack exposed 12 million emails
item.description:
The company said the breach affected an email system used to manage customer email accounts, webmail services and email storage for five Japanese internet service providers.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Britain plans to build autonomous AI 'Cyber Shield' to defend nation
item.description:
The capability, called Cyber Shield, is designed to counter a threat the National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) said could see attackers “move at machine speed and greater scale, reducing opportunities for detection and response.”
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
item.description:
Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and a large number of internal CISA systems. Security experts said the public archive included files detailing how CISA builds, tests and deploys software internally, and that it represents one of the most egregious government data leaks in recent history.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
item.description:
Lawmakers in both houses of Congress are demanding answers from the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) after KrebsOnSecurity reported this week that a CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and a vast trove of other agency secrets on a public GitHub account. The inquiry comes as CISA is still struggling to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
item.description:
Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations and disinformation campaigns inside the European Union. The two men were the focus of a 2025 KrebsOnSecurity story about how their hosting companies had assumed control over the technical infrastructure of Stark Industries Solutions, an Internet service provider sanctioned last year by the EU as a frequ
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
item.description:
The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on Telegram showing how to trick Meta's "AI support assistant" bot into resetting account passwords.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Who Runs the Ransomware Group ‘The Gentlemen?’
item.description:
A cybercrime group known as The Gentlemen has emerged as the second most active ransomware gang by victim count, rapidly attracting a talented pool of hackers through an aggressive recruitment strategy that promises affiliates 90 percent of any ransom paid by victims. This post examines clues pointing to a real life identity for the administrator of The Gentlemen ransomware group.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
item.description:
Two men pleaded guilty in the United Kingdom this week to criminal charges stemming from an August 2024 cyberattack that crippled Transport for London, the entity responsible for the public transport network in the Greater London area. The duo were key members of a prolific cybercrime group known as Scattered Spider, and their guilty pleas came on the first day of what was expected to be a six-week trial.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Employees Drop Class Action Lawsuit Against Stryker Over Hamdala Cyberattack
item.description:
A consolidated class action lawsuit against the medtech company Stryker over a March 2026 cyberattack has been voluntarily dismissed by […] The post Employees Drop Class Action Lawsuit Against Stryker Over Hamdala Cyberattack appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Delaware & Florida Women’s Health Centers Announce Data Breaches
item.description:
Two women’s healthcare providers have announced data privacy incidents. Women’s Wellness of Southern Delaware recently learned about unauthorized retention of […] The post Delaware & Florida Women’s Health Centers Announce Data Breaches appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: AdaptHealth Reports Material Cybersecurity Incident and Theft of Patient Data
item.description:
AdaptHealth, a publicly traded healthcare company that provides home medical equipment, diabetes supplies, and sleep therapy products, has informed the […] The post AdaptHealth Reports Material Cybersecurity Incident and Theft of Patient Data appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Almost 30,000 Texas Residents Affected by Data Breach at The Texas Hearing Institute
item.description:
The Texas Hearing Institute has notified the Texas Attorney General about a data breach impacting more than 29, 000 state […] The post Almost 30,000 Texas Residents Affected by Data Breach at The Texas Hearing Institute appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: AI Agent Conducts First Fully Autonomous Ransomware Attack
item.description:
Researchers have identified what they believe to be the first agentic ransomware attack. An autonomous large language model (LLM) agent […] The post AI Agent Conducts First Fully Autonomous Ransomware Attack appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: North Los Angeles County Regional Center Notifies Individuals About November 2024 Ransomware Attack
item.description:
North Los Angeles County Regional Center has started mailing notification letters to individuals affected by a November 2024 ransomware attack, […] The post North Los Angeles County Regional Center Notifies Individuals About November 2024 Ransomware Attack appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Small Practice Owners Guide to HIPAA Compliance Programs
item.description:
Article Summary Ownership carries HIPAA responsibility regardless of delegation because the practice answers to the Office for Civil Rights rather […] The post Small Practice Owners Guide to HIPAA Compliance Programs appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Calibrated Healthcare Settles Class Action Data Breach Lawsuit
item.description:
Calibrated Healthcare Systems, LLC, and Calibrated Healthcare, LLC, have agreed to settle a class action lawsuit stemming from a February […] The post Calibrated Healthcare Settles Class Action Data Breach Lawsuit appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Free Webinar TODAY: AI + HIPAA: Innovating in Healthcare Without Leaving Compliance Behind
item.description:
Artificial intelligence has tremendous potential in healthcare, and healthcare organizations have embraced AI tools in all areas of their operation; […] The post Free Webinar TODAY: AI + HIPAA: Innovating in Healthcare Without Leaving Compliance Behind appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Webinar tomorrow: Why modern email attacks require a new approach to defense
item.description:
Tomorrow's webinar explores how behavioral AI can help organizations detect sophisticated phishing, business email compromise, and account takeover attacks while reducing alert fatigue through automated investigation and response workflows. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: The GitHub Actions Attack Pattern Your CI Security Scanners Miss
item.description:
ActiveState explains how GitHub Actions attack chains can evade traditional CI security scanners, why passing a scan doesn't guarantee a secure pipeline, and how organizations can better govern their CI/CD workflows. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Spain arrests suspected member of pro-Russian hacktivist groups
item.description:
The National Police in Spain have arrested a man who is suspected of being an active member of the CyberArmy of Russia Reborn (CARR) and Z-Pentest, both pro-Russian hacktivist groups. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Chinese hackers develop LONGLEASH malware to expand ORB network
item.description:
Chinese hackers tracked as 'UAT-7810' are actively evolving their malware to expand their Operational Relay Box (ORB) network by compromising internet-facing networking devices, primarily unpatched Ruckus routers. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Accenture confirms breach after hacker offers stolen data for sale
item.description:
IT services giant Accenture has confirmed it suffered a security breach after a threat actor claimed to have stolen 35 GB of source code and other data from the company. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: UK journalists and NGOs risk terrorism prosecutions under new security bill
item.description:
MEE reports: New national security legislation being rushed through the UK’s parliament could criminalise British foreign correspondents and NGO workers engaging with designated state-backed groups, experts warn. The National Security (State Threats) Bill, which is moving through its final stages in parliament this week, hands the UK Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood sweeping powers to designate as a threat any... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Global Schools Holdings Cites Two Injunctions in a Bid to Chill Our Reporting. It Won’t Work.
item.description:
My About page is pretty clear about legal threats: If you want to send me legal threats about my reporting or comments, knock yourself out, but don’t be surprised to see me report on your threat, any confidentiality sig blocks you may attach notwithstanding. I have been threatened with lawsuits many times, and to be... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: HK: Shun Hing Group data breach affects 920,000 customers, 1.05m files encrypted in cyber attack
item.description:
Erwin Wong reports: Shun Hing Group has confirmed that its computer systems were compromised by hackers in March, resulting in a significant data breach affecting customers and staff. Founded in 1953 by the late Dr William Mong, Shun Hing Group has grown into a leading and diversified Hong Kong conglomerate, best known as the sole... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: An AI just carried out a cyber attack without any human oversight for the first time
item.description:
Anthony Cuthbertson reports: Security researchers have uncovered what they believe to be the first ever instance of an artificial intelligence agent executing a cyber attack from start to finish without human assistance. The AI-powered attack marks a major milestone for both artificial intelligence and cyber security, raising concerns that AI is lowering the barrier for cyber criminals. The fully automated campaign involved... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: AdaptHealth says attackers sweet-talked their way into cloud systems and stole patient data
item.description:
Connor Jones reports: AdaptHealth says attackers used social engineering to breach its systems and steal sensitive patient data, including passwords associated with insurance billing. The medical equipment company disclosed the attack to the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) on Thursday, noting that attackers accessed internal patient management systems, document storage platforms, and external electronic health record system... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Alberta, Centurion Project sued over alleged data breach that affected millions of voters
item.description:
Carrie Tait reports: A retired lawyer is suing Alberta, its Chief Electoral Officer and two organizations that support secession for their respective roles in an alleged data breach affecting 2.9 million residents in the province. Clint Docken, a former class-action lawyer, last week filed a sweeping lawsuit stemming from allegations the Centurion Project unlawfully obtained... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: The “Anonymous” Tip System That Wasn’t: Three Months Later, Why Hasn’t Navigate360 Notified Anyone?
item.description:
Trigger Warning: This post includes content from tips submitted to anonymous tiplines by or about students. While identity information is redacted, tips may include obscenities and explicit references to sexual abuse, rape, assault, self-harm, violence, suicidal ideation, pornography, and pedophilia. Overview On March 18, 2026, DDoSecrets and Straight Arrow News reported on a dataset provided... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Jacksonville, Texas, keeps some city systems offline after cyber incident
item.description:
DysruptionHub reports: Jacksonville, Texas, took some city systems offline after detecting suspicious network activity Friday, leaving some online services unavailable Monday as officials investigated a cybersecurity incident. The city said it detected the activity July 3 and later determined it was related to a cybersecurity incident. Officials took affected systems offline, disabled some systems as... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Bojangles sued again by workers over Russian hacker data breach. NC judge weighs in
item.description:
Chase Jordan reports an update in the litigation stemming from a 2024 breach by Hunters International. This case has raised a number of issues about standing and negligence and has been up and down in the courts, with plaintiffs seeming to fare better in state court: A class-action lawsuit over a 2024 data breach of Bojangles employee... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Washington Dept. of Social and Health Services announces massive data breach
item.description:
KIRO7 reports: The Washington Department of Social and Health Services (DSHS) is issuing a notice of a massive data breach that happened in March, potentially compromising the personal data of around 8,600 people. An internal investigation revealed that a former DSHS employee accessed the data without authorization. Some of the data the employee improperly potentially... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Keystone Homes Report Victim gokeystone.com Threat Actor Qilin Date Discovered Jul 07, 2026 Description Keystone Homes is a residential construction company based in the USA (North â¦July 07, 2026
item.description:
Keystone Homes Report Victim gokeystone.com Threat Actor Qilin Date Discovered Jul 07, 2026 Description Keystone Homes is a residential construction company based in the USA (North â¦July 07, 2026 reported a ransomware. Attributed to Chaos.
🎭 Threat Actor: Chaos | 📰 Source: BreachSense | 🏷️ Type: Ransomware

item.title: Ingram Content Group Report Victim ingramcontent.com Threat Actor ShinyHunters Date Discovered Jul 07, 2026 Description Ingram Content Group is a publishing services company based in the â¦July 07, 2026
item.description:
